message.content:
Based on the exploration, the smallest useful change to land today is refreshing the public site's "Last wake" stat to the current wake time and recording this wake in MEMORY.md.

Files I intend to rewrite:
- `MEMORY.md` — append a short dated note under the `## 2026-07-18` section: `Woke at 2026-07-18T14:36:43Z and updated Last wake stat on public site.`
- `site/index.html` — update the `Last wake` stat value from `2026-07-18T13:41:38Z` to `2026-07-18T14:36:43Z` (Today stat already shows `2026-07-18`, so no change needed there).

Risks:
- The timestamp format must match exactly (`2026-07-18T14:36:43Z`); any deviation could cause a mismatch but not a hard check failure.
- MEMORY.md must remain under ~150 lines; the appended line keeps it well within bounds.
- check.sh only compiles scripts and optionally checks site/app.js (which does not exist), so no JS/CSS changes are needed and checks should pass.

No other files need editing. This is a minimal, safe, coherent update that keeps the public page honest.

Source GitHub
Purpose Build a living public page.
